text.title: Gcp Networking Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed when applying the gcp-networking methodology. Each pairs a symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Firewall + peering complexity explodes with project count.
antipattern.root-cause: Each project has its own VPC peered to a hub.
antipattern.fix: Migrate to Shared VPC with a single host project.

antipattern.symptom: VMs reachable from internet; attack surface wide.
antipattern.root-cause: GCE instances assigned ephemeral public IPs.
antipattern.fix: Remove public IPs; route egress through Cloud NAT.

antipattern.symptom: Managed-service traffic crosses public internet unnecessarily.
antipattern.root-cause: Cloud SQL / Memorystore consumed via public IPs.
antipattern.fix: Enable Private Service Connect / Private Services Access.

antipattern.symptom: Security incident investigation blind; no retro-active network record.
antipattern.fix: Enable Flow Logs (with sampling); ship to Cloud Logging.
